config/schema/root.ts and the friendly authoring view in config/index.ts.
SDK consumers and CLI loaders had to cast (`as I18nPruneConfig`) to bridge

createCoreContext.

Inline the bridge into the originals so there's a single public
I18nPruneConfig (the friendly view) that's still zod-validated:

- schema/root.ts: rename the local z.infer<> alias to I18nPruneConfigParsed
  (internal name used by defaults/app.ts + define.ts spread bodies).
  parseI18nPruneConfig now returns the friendly I18nPruneConfig, with rich
  JSDoc documenting when to prefer it over loadCoreConfigFromPath.
- schema/define.ts: defineConfig parameter and return retyped against the
  friendly I18nPruneConfig (type-only cycle via `import type`, safe at
  runtime). Rich JSDoc with usage example, the "no .json" note, and pointer
  to parseI18nPruneConfig for runtime-validated input.
- defaults/app.ts: DEFAULT_CONFIG typed as I18nPruneConfigParsed (internal).
- schema/index.ts: re-export the renamed internal type.
- config/index.ts: friendly I18nPruneConfig now Omit<I18nPruneConfigParsed, ...>;
  defineConfig / parseI18nPruneConfig re-exported directly from
  schema/index.js (no wrapper functions).
- cli/shared/config/load.ts: drop the now-redundant `as I18nPruneConfig` cast.
- Two test assertions adjusted: c.translate?.policy.routing → c.translate?.policy?.routing

subject: wire onIncomplete + onHandoffPick in runGenerate (5.b.3.hooks.wire)
body:
runGenerate gains an optional 4th argument `hooks?: GenerateRunHooks`:

- onHandoffPick: between providers, when a retryable failure leaves >=1
  provider untried, the host can pin the next provider id from
  `remainingProviderIds`. Returning null keeps the natural chain order.
  Invalid pick (id not in remaining) throws I18nPruneError(USAGE).

- onIncomplete: post-loop, when no provider produced a result. Reason is
  `provider_chain_exhausted` (retryable terminal) or `partial_after_non_
  retryable`. Default decision (`hooks` undefined or returns undefined):
  `abort_no_write` -> rethrow lastErr (today's exact behavior).

  `write_partial` and `retry_provider` are accepted by the type contract
  but throw I18nPruneError(USAGE) until 5.b.3.hooks.behavior wires the
  partial-write path.

Refactor: in-catch terminal `throw rootCause` -> `break` so the
post-loop block owns the consent point. Provider order made mutable
(`chain`) so onHandoffPick can swap the next slot in place.

Parity-byte-identical: CLI does not supply hooks; existing snapshot
unchanged. 467/467 tests pass.

subject: add runTranslate SDK primitive (slice 5.b.2.b/c)
body:
Public SDK entry that orchestrates the full translator pipeline — provider
chain + retries + identity guard + parallelism + rate-limit math — for plain
strings or keyed leaves. SDK consumers call it directly; runGenerate (next
slice) will use it internally with hooks tuned for "generate a full target
locale".

  ProviderAttemptReport, TranslateHooks, TranslateIdentityGuardOptions.

Implementation (5.b.2.c):
- packages/core/src/translator/run.ts: runTranslate.
  - texts XOR leaves (output preserves input order; key echoed back when set).
  - Whitespace-only sources skip the network as ok=false / reason=skipped.
  - Per-provider chain from resolveTranslateConfig; retryable failures advance,
    non-retryable throws (issues attached by translateLeaf carry through).
  - Partial resume across providers: completed leaves carry over, next
    provider only retranslates still-missing entries.
  - Identity-streak guard opt-in via identityGuard.enabled; warning issues
    surface on output.issues. Hosts that need a confirm prompt keep owning
    their own guard via hooks.onTranslatedLeaf.
  - Hooks: onTick (progress), onProviderAttempt (attempt observer),
    onTranslatedLeaf (per-leaf side effects).
